---
title: "Sixes Classics team builder"
subtitle: "Monte Carlo simulation-based fantasy cycling team optimiser"
format: html
---

# Configuration

```{julia}
#| echo: true
#| output: false
using Velogames, DataFrames, Statistics, MarkdownTables

# Race — use get_url_pattern("name") to check available names
race_name = "strade"
race_year = 2026
racehash = "#StradeBianche"  # VG startlist hash filter (leave empty for all riders)

# Optional data sources (leave empty to skip)
betfair_market_id = ""  # Betfair Exchange market ID from the URL
oracle_url = ""

# Qualitative intelligence (leave both empty to skip)
qualitative_youtube_url = ""    # YouTube URL for auto-extraction via yt-dlp + Claude API
qualitative_json_file = ""      # Path to manually saved JSON response file

# Simulation
n_sims = 1000         # Monte Carlo iterations (more = smoother, slower)
history_years = 5     # Years of PCS race history to fetch
risk_aversion = 1.5   # Penalise high-variance riders (0 = risk-neutral, higher = more conservative)
domestique_discount = 0.5  # Penalise non-leaders proportionally to strength gap (0 = off)
max_per_team = 0      # Max riders from any single team (0 = no limit)

# Exclusions
excluded_riders = String[]

# Cache
race_cache = CacheConfig(joinpath(homedir(), ".velogames_cache"), 6)

# Helper: print a MarkdownTables table as raw markdown text
md_table(df) = print(sprint(show, MIME("text/markdown"), markdown_table(df)))
```

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Using qualitative intelligence

Two ways to incorporate expert assessments from podcasts, articles, or pre-race previews.

**Automatic** (requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in `.envrc` and `yt-dlp` installed): set `qualitative_youtube_url` to a YouTube video URL. The pipeline fetches auto-captions via yt-dlp, sends them to the Claude API for structured extraction, and feeds the result into the Bayesian model.

**Manual** (no API key needed):

1. Run `build_qualitative_prompt(riders, race_name, date)` to generate a prompt containing the startlist — paste the output into any LLM chat (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.) along with your source material
2. Save the JSON array response to a file
3. Set `qualitative_json_file` to the file path

To get the rider list for the prompt before running the full prediction:

```julia
config = suppress_output() do
    setup_race(race_name, race_year; cache_config=race_cache)
end
riders = suppress_output() do
    getvgriders(config.current_url; cache_config=race_cache)
end
prompt = build_qualitative_prompt(String.(riders.rider), race_name, string(Dates.today()))
clipboard(prompt)  # or print(prompt)
```

The expected JSON format is an array of rider assessments:

```json
[
  {"rider": "Mathieu van der Poel", "category": "moderate_positive", "confidence": "high", "reasoning": "Won recent Kuurne, top cobbled form."},
  {"rider": "Wout van Aert", "category": "slight_negative", "confidence": "medium", "reasoning": "Returning from injury, unclear fitness."}
]
```

Categories: `strong_positive`, `moderate_positive`, `slight_positive`, `neutral`, `slight_negative`, `moderate_negative`, `strong_negative`. Confidence: `high`, `medium`, `low`. Only include riders where you have specific intelligence.
:::

# Prediction

```{julia}
#| output: asis

config = suppress_output() do
    setup_race(race_name, race_year; cache_config=race_cache)
end
scoring = get_scoring(config.category > 0 ? config.category : 2)

# --- Load qualitative intelligence (optional) ---
qualitative_df = nothing
if !isempty(qualitative_youtube_url)
    try
        riders_tmp = suppress_output() do
            getvgriders(config.current_url; cache_config=race_cache)
        end
        qualitative_df = get_qualitative_auto(
            qualitative_youtube_url,
            String.(riders_tmp.rider),
            race_name,
            string(Dates.today()),
        )
        @info "Qualitative intelligence: $(nrow(qualitative_df)) rider assessments extracted"
    catch e
        @warn "Failed to extract qualitative intelligence: $e"
    end
elseif !isempty(qualitative_json_file)
    try
        qualitative_df = load_qualitative_file(qualitative_json_file)
        @info "Qualitative intelligence: $(nrow(qualitative_df)) rider assessments loaded"
    catch e
        @warn "Failed to load qualitative file: $e"
    end
end

predicted, chosenteam = suppress_output() do
    solve_oneday(config;
        racehash=racehash,
        history_years=history_years,
        betfair_market_id=betfair_market_id,
        oracle_url=oracle_url,
        n_sims=n_sims,
        excluded_riders=excluded_riders,
        qualitative_df=qualitative_df,
        risk_aversion=risk_aversion,
        domestique_discount=domestique_discount,
        max_per_team=max_per_team)
end

# Archive prediction snapshot for retrospective analysis in team_assessor
if nrow(predicted) > 0 && !isempty(config.pcs_slug)
    save_race_snapshot(predicted, "prediction", config.pcs_slug, config.year)
end

if nrow(predicted) == 0
    print("**No riders found.** Check race name, year, and startlist hash filter.\n")
    error("No riders — cannot continue")
end

# --- Race summary ---
n_total = nrow(predicted)
ra_str = risk_aversion > 0 ? "γ=$(risk_aversion)" : "risk-neutral"
print("""
**$(titlecase(config.name)) $(config.year)** — Category $(config.category), $(n_total) riders, $(n_sims) simulations, $(ra_str)

""")

# --- Data sources ---
n_pcs = count(predicted.has_pcs)
n_history = count(predicted.has_race_history)
n_odds = count(predicted.has_odds)
n_oracle = count(predicted.has_oracle)
n_vg_hist = count(predicted.has_vg_history)
n_qualitative = count(predicted.has_qualitative)
n_form = count(predicted.has_form)
n_seasons = count(predicted.has_seasons)
pct(n) = round(Int, 100 * n / n_total)

print("""
::: {.callout-note collapse="false"}
## Data sources

| Source | Coverage | Status |
|--------|----------|--------|
| PCS specialty ratings | $(n_pcs)/$(n_total) riders ($(pct(n_pcs))%) | Observation |
| VG season points | $(n_total)/$(n_total) riders (100%) | Always used |
| PCS form (6 weeks) | $(n_form)/$(n_total) riders ($(pct(n_form))%) | $(n_form > 0 ? "Active" : "No data") |
| Career trajectory | $(n_seasons)/$(n_total) riders ($(pct(n_seasons))%) | $(n_seasons > 0 ? "Active" : "No data") |
| PCS race history ($(history_years) yrs) | $(n_history)/$(n_total) riders ($(pct(n_history))%) | $(n_history > 0 ? "Active" : "No matches") |
| Similar races | $(let sr = get(SIMILAR_RACES, config.pcs_slug, String[]); isempty(sr) ? "None configured" : join(sr, ", "); end) | $(isempty(get(SIMILAR_RACES, config.pcs_slug, String[])) ? "N/A" : "Included with higher variance") |
| VG race history | $(n_vg_hist)/$(n_total) riders ($(pct(n_vg_hist))%) | $(n_vg_hist > 0 ? "Active" : "No matches") |
| Cycling Oracle | $(n_oracle)/$(n_total) riders ($(pct(n_oracle))%) | $(isempty(oracle_url) ? "Not provided" : (n_oracle > 0 ? "Active" : "No matches")) |
| Qualitative intel | $(n_qualitative)/$(n_total) riders ($(pct(n_qualitative))%) | $(qualitative_df === nothing ? "Not provided" : (n_qualitative > 0 ? "Active" : "No matches")) |
| Betfair odds | $(n_odds)/$(n_total) riders ($(pct(n_odds))%) | $(isempty(betfair_market_id) ? "Not provided" : (n_odds > 0 ? "Active" : "No matches")) |

Signals are combined via Bayesian updating from an uninformative prior (mean 0, variance 100). PCS specialty is the broadest observation; race-specific history and VG season form are moderate signals; Cycling Oracle predictions and betting odds are the strongest when available.
:::

""")

# --- Signal impact ---
rms(v) = sqrt(mean(v .^ 2))
signal_names = ["PCS specialty", "VG season points", "PCS form", "Trajectory", "PCS race history", "VG race history", "Cycling Oracle", "Qualitative intel", "Betfair odds"]
shift_cols = [:shift_pcs, :shift_vg, :shift_form, :shift_trajectory, :shift_history, :shift_vg_history, :shift_oracle, :shift_qualitative, :shift_odds]
affected_counts = [count(!=(0.0), predicted[!, c]) for c in shift_cols]
rms_shifts = [rms(predicted[!, c]) for c in shift_cols]

print("""
::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Signal impact

How much each source shifted rider strength estimates from the uninformative prior (root mean square shift across all riders).

| Signal | Riders affected | RMS shift |
|--------|-----------------|-----------|
""")
for (name, n_aff, rmss) in zip(signal_names, affected_counts, rms_shifts)
    print("| $name | $n_aff | $(round(rmss, digits=3)) |\n")
end
print("""

Higher RMS shift means the signal had more influence on the final estimates. A shift of 0.0 means the signal was not available or had no effect.
:::

""")
```

# Your optimal team

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if @isdefined(chosenteam) && nrow(chosenteam) > 0
    total_cost = sum(chosenteam.cost)
    total_evg = sum(chosenteam.expected_vg_points)

    print("""
**Total cost:** $(total_cost) / 100 credits | **Expected VG points:** $(round(total_evg, digits=1)) | **Budget remaining:** $(100 - total_cost)

""")

    display_cols = [:rider, :team, :cost, :expected_vg_points, :std_vg_points, :strength, :uncertainty]
    if risk_aversion > 0
        insert!(display_cols, findfirst(==(:std_vg_points), display_cols) + 1, :risk_adjusted_vg_points)
    end
    for col in [:expected_finish_pts, :expected_assist_pts, :expected_breakaway_pts]
        if col in propertynames(chosenteam)
            push!(display_cols, col)
        end
    end
    display_cols = filter(c -> c in propertynames(chosenteam), display_cols)

    team_display = chosenteam[:, display_cols]
    round_numeric_columns!(team_display)
    clean_team_names!(team_display, [:team])
    md_table(sort(team_display, :expected_vg_points, rev=true))
    print("\n")
else
    print("""
::: {.callout-warning}
No optimal team generated — check configuration and try again.
:::
""")
end
```

# Full prediction rankings

Top 30 riders by expected VG points:

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if @isdefined(predicted) && nrow(predicted) > 0
    ranking_cols = [:rider, :team, :cost, :expected_vg_points, :std_vg_points, :strength, :uncertainty]
    if risk_aversion > 0
        insert!(ranking_cols, findfirst(==(:std_vg_points), ranking_cols) + 1, :risk_adjusted_vg_points)
    end
    for col in [:expected_finish_pts, :expected_assist_pts, :expected_breakaway_pts]
        if col in propertynames(predicted)
            push!(ranking_cols, col)
        end
    end
    if :chosen in propertynames(predicted)
        push!(ranking_cols, :chosen)
    end
    ranking_cols = filter(c -> c in propertynames(predicted), ranking_cols)

    ranking = sort(predicted[:, ranking_cols], :expected_vg_points, rev=true)
    top_n = min(30, nrow(ranking))
    ranking_display = ranking[1:top_n, :]
    round_numeric_columns!(ranking_display)
    clean_team_names!(ranking_display, [:team])
    md_table(ranking_display)
    print("\n")
end
```

# Alternative picks

```{julia}
#| output: asis
if @isdefined(predicted) && @isdefined(chosenteam) && nrow(chosenteam) > 0
    not_chosen = filter(:chosen => ==(false), predicted)

    if nrow(not_chosen) > 0
        not_chosen[!, :value] = not_chosen.expected_vg_points ./ not_chosen.cost

        # Value picks
        print("### Best value not selected\n\n")
        print("Riders with the highest expected points per credit, not in the optimal team.\n\n")
        top_value = sort(not_chosen, :value, rev=true)[1:min(10, nrow(not_chosen)), :]
        value_display = top_value[:, [:rider, :team, :cost, :expected_vg_points, :value]]
        round_numeric_columns!(value_display)
        clean_team_names!(value_display, [:team])
        md_table(value_display)
        print("\n\n")

        # High-upside picks
        print("### High upside\n\n")
        print("Strong riders with high uncertainty — potential outperformers if conditions suit them.\n\n")
        not_chosen[!, :upside] = not_chosen.strength .+ not_chosen.uncertainty
        upside = sort(not_chosen, :upside, rev=true)[1:min(5, nrow(not_chosen)), :]
        upside_display = upside[:, [:rider, :team, :cost, :expected_vg_points, :strength, :uncertainty]]
        round_numeric_columns!(upside_display)
        clean_team_names!(upside_display, [:team])
        md_table(upside_display)
        print("\n\n")

        # Budget options
        print("### Budget options\n\n")
        cheap_options = filter(row -> row.cost <= 6, not_chosen)
        if nrow(cheap_options) > 0
            print("Best riders costing 6 credits or less.\n\n")
            cheap_sorted = sort(cheap_options, :expected_vg_points, rev=true)[1:min(5, nrow(cheap_options)), :]
            cheap_display = cheap_sorted[:, [:rider, :team, :cost, :expected_vg_points]]
            round_numeric_columns!(cheap_display)
            clean_team_names!(cheap_display, [:team])
            md_table(cheap_display)
            print("\n")
        else
            print("No riders at cost 6 or below available.\n\n")
        end
    end
end
```
